curation_notes: The vulnerability is in lib/bson.js in the toBSON function, which uses vm.runInNewContext('doc = eval((' + string + '));', sandbox) to parse user-provided BSON strings. An authenticated user can escape the VM sandbox via prototype chain traversal (this.constructor.constructor) to execute arbitrary OS commands. The patch replaces this unsafe eval-based parsing with the mongodb-query-parser library. Pre-assigned class command-injection is correct: CWE-78 (OS Command Injection) applies because the PoC demonstrates arbitrary command execution via child_process.execSync. No sibling files contain the same vulnerable pattern. lib/json.js was deleted and lib/routes/collection.js had a minor refactor to use the new parseObjectId function, but the core vulnerability is solely in toBSON.
